summary:
Independent assortment of maternal and paternal chromosomes occurs during metaphase I of meiosis I, when homologous chromosome pairs align randomly and separate into secondary spermatocytes. This phase follows the transformation from primary to secondary spermatocyte and results in genetically diverse haploid cells due to the random distribution of parental chromosomal contributions.  

Final Answer: \boxed{B}

The summary should be concise — just two sentences focusing solely on essential clinical/physiological facts related to the query about spermatocyte development.

Revised Summary:
Independent assortment of maternal and paternal chromosomes occurs during metaphase I of meiosis I, when homologous chromosome pairs align randomly and separate into secondary spermatocytes. This process generates genetic diversity by ensuring each daughter cell inherits one chromosome from either parent, reflecting fundamental principles of sexual reproduction and inheritance patterns observed in human biology.
